resources.string: Check your <a href=%1$s>AdGuard account</> to find your activation code
License canceled. Try another code or buy a new license
License expired. Try another code or buy a new license
Device limit reached. Remove unused devices from your AdGuard account or buy a new license
"This license doesn't exist. Try another code"
Something went wrong. Please try again or contact support
Bind this license to your AdGuard account to manage it even if the activation code gets lost or stolen
Your license will be removed from this device
Failed to reset license. Please try again or contact support
Ad blocking in apps, Tracking protection, Browsing Security, and Custom filters are not available in the free version

Shields up! Enjoy advanced protection until %s

Pro tip: Want to use your trial on other devices? Just log in to AdGuard there with your email address

Enjoy advanced protection!

Pro tip: Want to use your trial on other devices? Just log in to AdGuard there with your email address
"This setting isn't applied because Ad blocking is disabled"
"Traffic won't be filtered for these apps. Ads and trackers won't be blocked"
All settings for these apps will be returned to their defaults. Compatible apps will be filtered
System, banking, and some other apps may work incorrectly
Ad blocking may interfere with the apps below
Filtering is enabled for all apps. Some apps may work incorrectly
Filtering is enabled for problem-free apps
Filtering is stopped for all apps
Block ads, trackers, and annoyances for these apps
"Ads won't be blocked on websites in specific languages"
Blocks ads on websites in specified languages
All settings in this section will be reset to their default values
Blocks ads in browsers and apps
Without Basic protection, most ads won’t be blocked
Blocks ads on websites in specified languages
Contains manually added rules and rules created via Recent activity
Give a higher priority to the rule
To add more than one rule, use the rule editor

"AdGuard and AdGuard VPN won't be able to work simultaneously. Ads and trackers won't be blocked when AdGuard VPN is on
Disabling integration here will also disable Integrated mode in your AdGuard VPN app"
AdGuard VPN and Ad Blocker apps are compatible and work together to protect you
AdGuard protects you from ads and trackers. Install AdGuard VPN to make you anonymous and your traffic inconspicuous and enjoy an extra level of security
AdGuard and AdGuard VPN can work together. To make them compatible, update AdGuard
AdGuard and AdGuard VPN can work together. To make them compatible, update AdGuard VPN
Update AdGuard to use it with AdGuard VPN
Update AdGuard VPN to use it with AdGuard
Get rid of ads everywhere: in browsers, games, and other apps
"AdGuard won't block ads, trackers, and annoyances on this website"
"AdGuard won't block ads, trackers, and annoyances on this website"
All allowlist rules will be permanently deleted
All allowlist rules will be disabled
All allowlist rules will be enabled
"AdGuard doesn't block ads, trackers, and annoyances on the websites below"
"This setting isn't applied because Ad blocking is disabled"
"For details on what AdGuard's annoyance filters block, see Filter policy. For third-party filters, visit their homepages"

You are about to enable one or more annoyance filters. They block elements that are either unrelated to website content or related but annoying and to your user experience. Website owners may consider these elements mandatory: if you block them, you may be violating their terms; some functionality of websites may not be available or may not work properly. You understand and agree that you are solely responsible to comply with the terms of use of websites you visit and that AdGuard is not responsible for your compliance with the terms of use of websites you visit using our products.
Please read carefully before enabling annoyance filters
Blocks cookie consent forms on websites
"Blocks irritating banners that promote websites' mobile apps"
Blocks annoying popups that are unnecessary for website functionality
Blocks social media icons, such as Share and Like, on websites
Blocks third-party widgets including online assistants and live support chats
None are blocked because you did not select any
Blocks annoying web elements such as cookie notices, social widgets, or in-page popups
Please check the link you entered
Activate it manually in Settings or contact your seller
AdGuard will no longer filter it
AdGuard will now filter this website
Quickly change filtering settings for a recent app or website
DNS server for Integration mode or outbound proxy
Enter a valid IPv4 or IPv6 address
Plain DNS server addresses, one per line
If you leave the field empty or no server responds, the unencrypted AdGuard DNS Non-filtering server will be used
Plain DNS server addresses, one per line

"Automatically selects a DNS server based on your AdGuard and device settings
For better privacy, switches to AdGuard or custom DNS server when using Integration mode or outbound proxy"
"This setting isn't applied because DNS protection is disabled"
"This setting isn't applied because Manual proxy is used"
"This setting isn't applied because Private DNS is selected in your device settings"
Automatic DNS is inactive because DNS protection is disabled
Automatic DNS is inactive because Manual proxy is used
Automatic DNS is inactive because Private DNS is selected in your device settings
"Couldn't recognize the task from the Action field"
Check the automation password and try again
Available in the full version
App and filter updates and website safety checks
App and filter updates and website safety checks
"AdGuard filters apps — their battery usage is sometimes attributed to AdGuard in your device's settings. Here's the info on battery usage for AdGuard only, other apps are not included"
Stay informed about dangerous and phishing websites with Browsing Security
👑 Unlock ad blocking in apps, Tracking protection, and Browsing Security. Tap to learn more
"Access to full version's features for a year or forever"
💻 📱 Install AdGuard on your desktop and tablet or share it with your loved ones. Tap to learn more
Protect up to 9 devices with one license
🎮 Sounds cool? Tap to learn more
👁 Protect yourself from companies that collect and sell user data. Tap to learn more
Dark or light, depending on your device settings
"If filters aren't updated properly, disable battery optimization for AdGuard"
Failed to open Settings. Try to access them manually
Relatively stable, may contain bugs. Frequently updated
Unstable, may contain bugs. Most frequently updated. New features appear here first
The most stable. Just enjoy using the app
"If the filter auto-update doesn't work as expected, try disabling battery optimization for AdGuard"
Protection may not work properly while the hotspot is active
You may encounter ads and trackers in these apps: %1$s
AdGuard may pause when using Samsung Pay
"AdGuard won't block ads and trackers in these browsers: %1$s"
"This setting isn't applied because DNS protection is disabled"
"This setting isn't applied because Manual proxy is used"
"This setting isn't applied because Private DNS is selected in your device settings"
IP address, sdns://, quic://, https://, h3:// or tls://
Allows to block and unblock domains manually by using DNS filtering syntax
"This setting isn't applied because Manual proxy is used"
"This setting isn't applied because Private DNS is selected in your device settings"
"This setting isn't applied because DNS filters are disabled"
Failed to reinstall extension. Please try again or contact support
"This setting isn't applied because Extensions are disabled"
Please try again or contact support
Save the changes before closing the editor?
Your license wasn’t found or something went wrong. Please try again or contact support
This custom filter will be removed: %s
Some filter rules can significantly alter your browsing experience and compromise your privacy. Make sure to only use filters from developers you trust
All settings in this section will be reset to their default values
"These settings aren't applied because Ad Blocking is disabled"
"These settings aren't applied because Annoyance blocking is disabled"
"These settings aren't applied because Language-specific ad blocking is disabled"
"These settings aren't applied because Tracking protection is disabled"
"These settings aren't applied because Ad blocking is disabled"
"These filters aren't active because Annoyance blocking is disabled"
This setting is available in the full version
"These filters aren't active because Language-specific ad blocking is disabled"
"These filters aren't active because Tracking protection is disabled"
Enter URL or local file path
Enter a valid URL or file path
You can find more filters at filterlists.com
To import custom filters, allow AdGuard to access your device storage
All settings in this section will be reset to their default values
Trusted filters can use powerful rule modifiers – be sure you use a filter from trusted developers
Block annoying web elements, such as cookie notices or in-page popups
Allows to use third-party filters with AdGuard
Block ads on websites in specified languages
"This group contains various filters that don't fit into other categories"
Block requests to phishing and malicious websites
Block social media elements such as Like and Share buttons
To activate this filter, enable Ad blocking
To activate this filter, enable Annoyance blocking
To activate this filter, enable Language-specific ad blocking
To activate this filter, enable Tracking protection
"These filters aren't active because Ad blocking is disabled"
"These filters aren't active because Annoyance blocking is disabled"
"These filters aren't active because Language-specific ad blocking is disabled"
"These filters aren't active because Tracking protection is disabled"
Sets of rules to block ads, trackers, and annoyances
Select an app to set its firewall rules
All custom firewall rules will be deleted. App connections will be managed by global firewall rules
Allows to manage Internet access for specific apps. These rules override global rules
"This setting isn't applied because Firewall is disabled"
Allows to manage Internet access for specific apps. These rules override global rules
Actions based on recent Internet activity
"This app or group of apps isn't routed through AdGuard"
Firewall permissions for this group of apps based on global and custom rules
Mobile data is allowed by custom rule
Mobile data is allowed by global rule
Mobile data is blocked by custom rule
Mobile data is blocked by global rule
Mobile data with screen off is allowed by custom rule
Mobile data with screen off is allowed by global rule
Mobile data with screen off is blocked by custom rule
Mobile data with screen off is blocked by global rule
Use mobile data with screen off
Roaming data is allowed by custom rule
Roaming data is allowed by global rule
Roaming data is blocked by custom rule
Roaming data is blocked by global rule
Firewall permissions for this app based on global and custom rules
These settings are inactive because Custom firewall rules are disabled
"This setting isn't applied because Custom firewall rules are disabled"
"This setting isn't applied because Firewall is disabled"
This app or group of apps isn’t routed through AdGuard
To manage this app or group of apps, route their traffic through AdGuard
Wi-Fi is allowed by custom rule
Wi-Fi is allowed by global rule
Wi-Fi is blocked by custom rule
Wi-Fi is blocked by global rule
Wi-Fi with screen off is allowed by custom rule
Wi-Fi with screen off is allowed by global rule
Wi-Fi with screen off is blocked by custom rule
Wi-Fi with screen off is blocked by global rule
Use mobile data with screen off
Allows to manage Internet access for all apps
"This setting isn't applied because Firewall is disabled"
%s was blocked from accessing mobile data
%s was blocked from accessing mobile data with screen off
%s was blocked from accessing roaming data
%s was blocked from accessing Wi-Fi with screen off
%s was blocked from accessing mobile data
%s was blocked from accessing mobile data with screen off
%s was blocked from accessing roaming data
%s was blocked from accessing Wi-Fi with screen off
Notifies you when selected apps try to use the Internet
"This setting isn't applied because Firewall is disabled"
Notifies you when selected apps try to use the Internet
No activity yet – continue using AdGuard to view quick actions
Actions based on recent Internet activity
"This setting isn't applied because "Firewall is disabled
"This setting isn't applied because Custom firewall rules are disabled"
To use Firewall, allow AdGuard to access app usage stats on your device
Blocking ads and trackers via proxy server
Keep using the Internet to view stats on blocked requests and saved data here
To resume it, disable your third-party VPN
"Ads and trackers aren't blocked. Tap to enable"
Failed to open device notification settings. Try to access them manually
Take your online security to the next level across all your devices. Unlock hidden features for an even better Internet experience
The app will switch to the system theme and language. Update and autostart settings will be set to defaults
Some ads aren’t blocked. Learn how to fix it

"To stay protected even when you close AdGuard, please allow it to run in the background
AdGuard doesn't use much battery — check your battery usage in Statistics"
Block ads and trackers in the background
Enjoy a safe and clean Internet!

"Without HTTPS filtering, most ads and trackers won't be blocked

You can disable or enable it at any moment in Settings → Network → HTTPS filtering"
AdGuard will display the protection status in the notification bar. This helps prevent the system from shutting down AdGuard in the background
AdGuard is already protecting you from ads and trackers. Leave everything as is, or fine-tune the app to your needs
Failed to open Settings. Try accessing them manually
Trial ended. Buy the full version
Applying settings. Give us a moment
Protection resumes after you close Samsung Pay
Protection resumes after you disable a third-party VPN
Failed to save the certificate. Please try again or contact support
To filter HTTPS traffic, AdGuard needs to install its certificate on your device. The certificate is generated and stored locally
Save the certificate to install it in the next step
Most websites and apps use HTTPS to encrypt your connection. Ads and trackers get mixed into the HTTPS traffic unless you let AdGuard filter it
Some ads can’t be blocked without HTTPS filtering

Without HTTPS filtering, most ads and trackers won’t be blocked

You can disable or enable it at any moment in Settings → Network → HTTPS filtering
Failed to open Settings. Try accessing them manually
Couldn’t install the certificate. Try again?
%1$d. Accept a security warning. Why trust AdGuard?
%1$d. Open Settings and paste the copied text in the search field
%1$d. Tap the %2$s file to install it

To find this setting manually, please follow these instructions:

%1$s

Then continue with step %2$d
Open your device settings → Security → More settings → Encryption and credentials → Install from storage → CA certificate
Open your device settings → Security → More settings → Encryption and credentials → Install from storage → %1$s
Open your device settings → Security → More settings → Encryption and credentials → Install from storage → CA certificate
Open your device settings → Security → More settings → Encryption and credentials → Install from storage → %1$s
Open your device settings → Security → More settings → Encryption and credentials → Install from storage → CA certificate
Open your device settings → Security → More settings → Encryption and credentials → Install from storage → %1$s
Open your device settings → Security → More settings → Encryption and credentials → Install from storage → CA certificate
Open your device settings → Security → More settings → Encryption and credentials → Install from storage → %1$s
Open your device settings → Security → More settings → Encryption and credentials → Install from storage → CA certificate
Open your device settings → Security → More settings → Encryption and credentials → Install from storage → %1$s
Open your device settings → Security & privacy → More security settings → Encryption & credentials → Install a certificate → CA certificate
Open your device settings → Security & privacy → More security settings → Encryption & credentials → Install a certificate → %1$s
Open your device settings → Security & privacy → More security & privacy → Encryption & Credentials → Install a certificate → CA certificate
Open your device settings → Security & privacy → More security & privacy → Encryption & Credentials → Install a certificate → %1$s
Open your device settings → Security & Lock screen → Encryption & Credentials → Install a certificate → CA certificate
Open your device settings → Security & Lock screen → Encryption & Credentials → Install a certificate → %1$s
Open your device settings → Password & security → System security → Credential storage → Install certificates from storage → CA certificate
Open your device settings → Password & security → System security → Credential storage → Install certificates from storage → %1$s
Open your device settings → Security & privacy → More security & privacy → Credential storage → Install certificates from storage → CA certificate
Open your device settings → Security & privacy → More security & privacy → Credential storage → Install certificates from storage → %1$s
Open your device settings → Security → Encryption & credentials → Install a certificate → CA certificate
Open your device settings → Security → Encryption & credentials → Install a certificate → %1$s
Open your device settings → Security → More security settings → Encryption & credentials → Install a certificate → CA certificate
Open your device settings → Security → More security settings → Encryption & credentials → Install a certificate → %1$s
Open your device settings → Biometrics and security → Other security settings → Install from device storage → CA certificate
Open your device settings → Biometrics and security → Other security settings → Install from device storage → %1$s
Open your device settings → Security and privacy → Other security settings → Install from device storage → CA certificate
Open your device settings → Security and privacy → Other security settings → Install from device storage → %1$s
Open your device settings → Security and Privacy → More security settings → Install from device storage → CA certificate
Open your device settings → Security and Privacy → More security settings → Install from device storage → %1$s
Open your device settings → Security → More security settings → Encryption & credentials → Install a certificate → CA certificate
Open your device settings → Security → More security settings → Encryption & credentials → Install a certificate → %1$s
Open your device settings → Security → Encryption and credentials → Install a certificate → CA certificate
Open your device settings → Security → Encryption and credentials → Install a certificate → %1$s
Open your device settings → Fingerprints, face data, and screen lock → Privacy → More security settings → Encryption & credentials → Install a certificate → CA certificate
Open your device settings → Fingerprints, face data, and screen lock → Privacy → More security settings → Encryption & credentials → Install a certificate → %1$s
Open your device settings → Security → Advanced → Encryption & credentials → Install a certificate → CA certificate
Open your device settings → Security → Advanced → Encryption & credentials → Install a certificate → %1$s
AdGuard filters HTTPS traffic locally on your device. Ads and trackers are blocked before they load, while all server connections stay encrypted and secure
Over the years, AdGuard has earned an impeccable reputation in privacy protection. It is trusted by millions of users and praised by the media
"Couldn't install certificate. Please try installing it manually"
"This setting isn't applied because HTTPS filtering is disabled"
All settings in this section will be reset to their default values
"HTTPS traffic won't be filtered for this website"
HTTPS traffic will be filtered for this website
HTTPS traffic is filtered everywhere except for these websites
HTTPS traffic is filtered only on these websites
"This setting isn't applied because HTTPS filtering is disabled"
All settings in this section will be reset to their default values
HTTPS filtering is stopped for all apps
System, banking, and some other apps may work incorrectly
HTTPS filtering may interfere with the apps below
HTTPS traffic is filtered in all apps. Some apps may work incorrectly
HTTPS traffic is filtered in problem-free apps
Filter HTTPS traffic for these apps
"This setting isn't applied because HTTPS filtering is disabled"
"This setting isn't applied because HTTPS filtering is disabled"
HTTPS traffic is filtered everywhere except for these websites
HTTPS traffic is filtered only on these websites
Filters HTTPS traffic only on websites specified in Website blocklist
Filters websites with Extended Validation certificates
"This setting isn't applied because HTTPS filtering is disabled"
"This setting isn't applied because HTTPS filtering is disabled"
Please check the import link and try again. If it doesn’t help, contact support
The imported settings will replace your current ones. Other settings won’t be affected
Unfortunately, an error occurred during this operation. Please contact support for more information.
An error occurred during this operation. Please try again later.
⚠️ Your device is not protected from trackers and malicious websites. Tap to manage your license
"👑 Don't lose the perks of the full version — tap to manage your license"
Stay protected from ads, trackers, and malicious websites — tap to manage your license
💚 Let AdGuard keep you safe — tap to manage your license
"Take a look at what we've achieved together:"
Your logging level is Debug. Tap to set it to Default
Use your license on your desktop and tablet or protect your loved ones online
Switch to clock mode for the time input.
Switch to text input mode for the time input.

"Your new app is ready, but we couldn't transfer your setting to the new version.
All settings were reset to defaults."
"We're preparing your app for its new version. It won't take long. Keep the app open — just in case"
"We've transferred your settings to the new version. Enjoy using AdGuard!"
Start date selection: %1$s – End date selection: %2$s
Tap to switch to Calendar view
Tap to switch to year view
Notifications about apps that may conflict with AdGuard
Notifications about license status and suggestions
Notifications about the status of AdGuard protection
Notifications about apps that may conflict with AdGuard
New version of AdGuard is now available
AdGuard v%1$s for Android is now available!
Remove cookie notifications, live chats, and newsletter signups

"Most ads and trackers are sent over the encrypted HTTPS protocol. To block them, AdGuard needs to install its certificate on your device
The traffic is filtered locally. AdGuard keeps the connection secure and encrypted"
Removes tracking parameters from page URLs. Limits the lifetime of third-party cookies
Blocks online trackers and analytics systems
Protect yourself from online trackers and analytics systems
Remove ads from your search engine results
Be alerted before you access phishing and malicious websites
Get rid of Like, Share, and other social buttons on websites
AdGuard creates a VPN right on your device and uses it to filter traffic locally and securely
"I accept AdGuard's EULA and Privacy Policy"
"I accept AdGuard's EULA and Privacy Policy"

"This data covers:

 • The use and performance of the app
 • The app's configuration settings

It helps us understand how users interact with the app and identify its popular features and usability issues.

This data is anonymous and only used internally. We never share it with third parties.

Privacy policy"
"To be clear: we collect as little data as possible to run our websites and apps. We don't share your data with anyone"
"1. Select a YouTube video 2. Tap Share and choose AdGuard Player 3. Watch it without interruptions in a built-in video player!"
Failed to access app alarm settings
This will help AdGuard check the filter update status in the background
Allow AdGuard to set alarms and reminders
This will help AdGuard update filters regularly
Allow AdGuard to run in the background
Failed to open Settings. Try to access them manually
To avoid auto-update errors, make sure you grant the following permissions
"All permissions granted. Auto-updates should run seamlessly"
" Integration impossible: one of the apps needs an upgrade"
" Integration failed. Please try again or contact support"
Allows simultaneous use of AdGuard and AdGuard VPN
Allows tasker apps to manage AdGuard
To export logs, allow AdGuard to access your device storage

Use only to record a bug at the request of our technical support

Extensive logging consumes your device resources
"Changing these settings can affect AdGuard's stability, security, and performance"
Checks if AdGuard gets shut down by the battery optimization system and tries to restart it
Disabled by default. Enabling may cause the app to malfunction

Filtering of this app is limited to avoid compatibility issues
Learn more
Filtering of this app is limited to avoid compatibility issues
Blocks ads and trackers in unencrypted traffic
The selected settings for %1$s will be reset to their default values
May cause the app to malfunction

Filtering of this app is limited to avoid compatibility issues
Learn more
Filtering of this app is limited to avoid compatibility issues
Internet access settings for this app

This app is in our HTTPS filtering exclusions. Using the feature may cause the app to malfunction
Learn more
Disabled by default. Enabling may cause the app to malfunction
App may malfunction on non-rooted devices when enabled
No HTTPS certificate in System Storage. App may malfunction when enabled. Install certificate
Blocks ads and trackers in encrypted traffic, if possible
"This setting isn't applied because HTTPS filtering is disabled"
Routes app traffic through a specified proxy server or AdGuard VPN
"This setting isn't applied because Proxy is disabled"
Enter a valid URL or file path
Replace it with the new one?
Extension with this name already exists
To import extensions, allow AdGuard to access your device storage
All settings in this section will be reset to their default values

All settings in this section will be reset to their default values

These customizations will be removed: %2$s

Extensions can access your device data

Use only extensions from trusted developers
Extensions can access your device data. Use only extensions from trusted developers
"To add a userscript or userstyle, click "Add extension
Buy the full version to unlock this feature
Allows adding userscripts and userstyles to extend AdGuard Ad Blocker’s functionality and improve your web experience
This userstyle has unsupported features. Please try another extension
Ad blocking may interfere with the apps below
"Traffic won't be filtered for these apps. Ads and trackers won't be blocked"
The selected settings for all apps will be reset to their default values
Routing is stopped for all apps
"AdGuard won't be able to block ads and trackers in these apps"
Problem-free apps are now routed through AdGuard
System, banking, and some other apps may work incorrectly
All apps are now routed through AdGuard. Some apps may work incorrectly
Routing traffic through AdGuard may interfere with the apps below
To block ads and trackers in apps as well, you need the full version
Filtering is enabled for all browsers
Buy the full version to block ads and trackers in apps
Filtering is enabled for all apps. Some apps may work incorrectly
Filtering is enabled for problem-free apps
Filtering is stopped for all apps
To block ads and trackers in apps as well, you need the full version
Route these apps through proxy or AdGuard VPN
This data is anonymous and only used internally. We never share it with third parties.
If the app crashes, our developers get the following information:
List of automation rules in AdGuard Knowledge Base

"Add this password to the intent's Extra field
(password: ******)"
Allows tasker apps to manage AdGuard
Lists of rules for blocking ads, trackers, and annoyances
"To export settings, please allow AdGuard to access your device's storage"
"To import settings, please allow AdGuard to access your device's storage"
AdGuard protection, stats collection, and app notifications will be stopped
Enter a valid domain or IP address
"This setting isn't applied because Proxy is disabled"
To use this proxy server, enable Proxy
Allows to route UDP traffic through the specified SOCKS5 proxy. Make sure your proxy supports UDP
Allows resolving DNS requests on the outbound proxy server
To export these settings in full, check Filters

Failed to compress data.
Please try again or contact support.
The settings were exported successfully to %s

An internal error occurred.
Please try again or contact support.
"To export settings, please allow AdGuard to access your device's storage"
AdGuard CA certificate(s) in the user storage
All settings in this section will be reset to their default values
App filtering, traffic routing, and app management
Sets of rules to block ads, trackers, and annoyances
Failed to install certificate. Please try again
AdGuard Intermediate CA: Installed, valid until %1s
AdGuard Intermediate CA: Obsolete. Requires reinstalling
AdGuard Personal CA: Can be moved
AdGuard Personal CA: Installed, valid until %1s
AdGuard Personal CA: Obsolete. Requires reinstalling
"1. Delete the expired certificate. 2. Reinstall the certificate into the user store. 3. Move it to the system store."
"1. Open Magisk → Modules and disable adguardcert or your certificate transfer module. This will move the AdGuard Personal CA from the system store to the user store. 2. Reboot the device."

1. Open Settings → Biometrics and security → Other security settings.
2. Tap User certificates and delete the AdGuard Intermediate CA.

Alternatively, you can reinstall the certificate without deleting the expired one

We recommend using the adguardcert module: it supports two certificates at the same time. This allows you to filter HTTPS traffic in browsers and apps

After disabling the module, reboot your device

"It is required to move the AdGuard Personal CA to the system store to filter HTTPS traffic in apps
After the transfer, the AdGuard Intermediate CA will also allow you to filter HTTPS traffic in Chromium-based browsers"

Rooted devices allow you to filter HTTPS traffic both in browsers and apps. To do this, you need to move the AdGuard Personal CA from the user store to the system store

To move the certificate, use the adguardcert module: this ensures a safe transfer. The adguardcert module requires installing a second certificate into the user store

After the transfer, the AdGuard Intermediate CA will also allow you to filter HTTPS traffic in Chromium-based browsers
It will allow AdGuard to filter HTTPS traffic in browsers
It will allow AdGuard to filter HTTPS traffic in apps

1. Download the latest release of the adguardcert Magisk module.
2. Open Magisk → Modules → Install from storage and select the downloaded adguardcert file. This will move the certificate from the user to the system store.
3. Reboot the device.
Install the AdGuard Intermediate CA into the user store
Install the AdGuard Personal CA into the user store
Move the AdGuard Personal CA to the system store
How to move the certificate to the system store
To filter HTTPS traffic in apps, you need root access
How to filter HTTPS in apps
Can be moved to the system store to filter HTTPS in apps
"System store Required to run the adguardcert module"
"System store Required to filter HTTPS in browsers and apps"
"User store Required to run the adguardcert module"
"User store Required to filter HTTPS in browsers"
Required to run the adguardcert module
Required to filter HTTPS in browsers
Filters traffic sent over the HTTPS protocol
HTTPS traffic is filtered for these apps
To import Firewall settings and some others, please allow AdGuard to access the app usage data on your device
Firewall and some other settings won’t work properly
To import HTTPS filtering settings, install the CA certificate to your device
To import these settings in full, check Filters
Enjoy a safe and clean Internet!
"We didn't find any settings to import"
Please select at least one setting
An unknown error occurred. Please retry with another file or contact support
This file probably has a wrong format. Please retry with another file or contact support
These settings were imported from an obsolete app version. Please retry with another file or contact support
"This setting isn't applied because DNS protection is disabled"
"To apply this setting, install AdGuard's security certificate"
To apply this setting, enable HTTPS filtering
"This setting isn't applied because Private DNS is selected in your device settings"
"This setting isn't applied because Manual proxy is used"
Displays debugging information to the browser console
0.0.0.0 for A requests (IPv4) and :: for AAAA requests (IPv6)
Enter a valid IPv4 or IPv6 address
Packages for which AdGuard should bypass QUIC traffic
Plain DNS server addresses, one per line
Package or UID, one per line. Use // for comments
Domains, one per line. Limited wildcards are allowed
IP ranges, one per line. Use // for comments
Port ranges, one per line. Use .. to indicate the range
Package names, one per line. Use // for comments
Response type for domains blocked by DNS rules based on adblock rule syntax
Blocks TLS Encrypted Client Hello (ECH) in DNS responses
Time to live (TTL) of the record for blocked domains
Response type for domains blocked by DNS rules based on adblock rule syntax
Use the following IP addresses for blocked A requests (IPv4) and AAAA requests (IPv6)
Please enter a valid IPv4 address
Please enter a valid IPv6 address
Response type for domains blocked by DNS rules based on hosts rule syntax
Bootstrap DNS for encrypted DNS upstreams
If this field is empty, Automatic DNS will be used
Automatically redirects DNS search domains to fallback upstreams, if available
Uses fallback upstream for all domains, including non-fallback ones
Sends a SERVFAIL response to the client if all upstreams, including fallback ones, fail to reply. If this setting is disabled, no response is sent to the client
Tests DNS upstreams before adding or updating custom DNS servers
Fallback DNS resolvers to use when the configured server is unavailable
Domains redirected to fallback upstreams, if available
Allows changing the Filter DNS requests setting in the outbound proxy settings
Show the Filter DNS requests setting
Filters encrypted DNS requests in addition to plain DNS requests
Response type for domains blocked by DNS rules based on hosts rule syntax
"This setting isn't applied because DNS protection is disabled"
"This setting isn't applied because DNS protection is disabled. Enable"
Timeout to use for each DNS request
For multiple upstreams, the fallback DNS resolver is only used after the timeouts of each upstream have expired
Uses HTTP/3 to speed up DNS query resolution for DNS-over-HTTPS upstreams
Encrypts ClientHellos if necessary. We recommend using an encrypted DNS server
Automatically checks the revocation status of SSL certificates
Failed to access alarms and reminders settings
Pauses protection when you open the Samsung Pay app
Permission to set alarms and reminders
We need this permission to enable protection when Samsung Pay is closed
Allow AdGuard to set alarms and reminders
We need this permission to know that Samsung Pay is open
Allow AdGuard to access app usage stats
We only use these permissions to pause protection when Samsung Pay is open. Privacy policy
Give us permission to detect Samsung Pay
Packages and UIDs to exclude from protection
Save databases to a ZIP archive for sharing with AdGuard developers
"Invalid entry. If you leave it as is, we'll use the last available valid entry"
"This setting isn't applied because DNS protection is disabled"
Filters DNS-over-HTTPS traffic without redirecting it to the local DNS proxy. Uses the DNS server specified in the browser settings
Failed to open Private DNS settings
"This setting isn't applied because Private DNS is selected in your device settings"
Redirects DNS-over-HTTPS requests to the local DNS proxy for filtering. Uses the DNS server selected in the app
Filters encrypted DNS requests in addition to plain DNS requests
Filters traffic sent over HTTP/3, the latest version of the HTTP protocol based on QUIC
"This setting isn't applied because AdGuard's certificate isn't installed"
"This setting isn't applied because HTTPS filtering is disabled"
Logs all filtered HTTP requests in HAR 1.2 format to the har subdirectory in the app cache directory. Can be analyzed with Fiddler
May slow down your device. Use for debugging purposes only
When on Wi-Fi, the gateway IP address is added to VPN routes
Include Wi-Fi gateway in VPN routes
IP address used to create a TUN interface
Filtering for these IP ranges is disabled
For simple networks, excludes LAN connections from filtering, when possible. May not work for complex networks
IP address used to create a TUN interface
Filters IPv6 networks if an IPv6 network interface is available
Filtering for these IP ranges is disabled
Idle time, in seconds, before starting keepalive probes. The default is 0. Set this to 20 if you have problems with NAT
Sends a keepalive probe after the specified time interval
"Sends a keepalive probe after a specified time interval Idle time: %d s Time between keepalive probes: %d s"
Time, in seconds, between keepalive probes for an unresponsive peer. The default is 0
"This setting isn't applied because Manual proxy or Automatic proxy is used"
"This setting isn't applied because Manual proxy is used"
This setting isn’t applied because Manual proxy or Automatic proxy is used. Configure routing
The value should be greater than 0
The first port should not be equal or greater than a second one
Please enter a valid port range
A port must be between %1$d and %2$d
"This setting isn't applied because Private DNS is selected in your device settings"
"This setting isn't applied because Proxy is disabled"
If the device connects to a different network, restarts protection to reconfigure Automatic proxy parameters
Reconfigure Automatic proxy when network changes
"This setting isn't applied because Local VPN or Manual proxy is used"
Writes information about blocked HTML elements to Recent activity
The maximum size of the data packet used in local VPN
A delay before trying to restore VPN protection after it is revoked by a third-party VPN app or by deleting the VPN profile
A delay before rescheduling the restoration of VPN protection after it is revoked by a third-party VPN app or by deleting the VPN profile
Reschedule delay for revoked VPN recovery
Show Developer tools on the main screen
Filters traffic sent over the HTTPS protocol
"Allows to route your device's traffic through a proxy server"
"Sets how your traffic will be filtered Currently used: Local VPN"
"Sets how your traffic will be filtered Currently used: Manual proxy port %s"
"Sets how your traffic will be filtered Currently used: Automatic proxy"
All apps won’t be routed through your specified proxy server
All installed apps will be routed through your specified proxy server
All settings for these apps will be returned to their defaults. Problem-free and new apps will be filtered
Proxy routing is disabled for all apps
All apps are routed through proxy
"This setting isn't applied because Proxy is disabled"
To route apps through proxy, enable Proxy
"The button below redirects you to your device's app store"
Install Orbot to use it with AdGuard
To use this setting, turn off integration with AdGuard VPN
"Failed to open your device's app store. Try installing Orbot manually"
Failed to integrate. Please check Orbot settings
Unable to connect to proxy over TCP
Unable to connect to proxy over UDP
"Allows to route your device's traffic through a proxy server"
"This setting isn't applied because Proxy is disabled"
To use this proxy server, enable Proxy
"Allows to route your device's traffic through a proxy server"
"This setting isn't applied because integration with AdGuard VPN is on"
General, advanced, low-level, filter, and extension settings will be reset to their default values
Please try to manually disable your certificate transfer module
To install a certificate directly, set Firefox as your default browser
Older Firefox versions allow you to install the certificate directly into the browser. Newer versions require alternative installation methods
Try again or delete them manually via your certificate transfer method

"1. Tap the button below to delete the AdGuard certificate(s) located in the system store. 2. Follow the instructions from the next step to delete the AdGuard certificate(s) located in the user store."

"1. Open Magisk → Modules and disable adguardcert or your certificate transfer module. This will move the AdGuard certificate(s) from the system store to the user store. 2. Reboot the device. 3. Open Settings → Biometrics and security → Other security settings. 4. Tap User certificates and delete the AdGuard certificate(s).
These instructions may differ for older or customized versions of Android. If you have trouble locating the certificates, use the search bar or contact the developers of your OS version"

"1. Open Settings → Biometrics and security → Other security settings. 2. Tap User certificates and delete the AdGuard certificate(s).
These instructions may differ for older or customized versions of Android. If you have trouble locating the certificates, use the search bar or contact the developers of your OS version."
Delete certificate(s) from the user store
Try moving it manually via Magisk
HTTPS traffic won’t be filtered in apps

"1. Download the latest release of the adguardcert Magisk module. 2. Open Magisk → Settings and enable Zygisk. 3. Return to Modules → Install from storage and select the downloaded adguardcert file. This will move the certificate from the user to the system store. 4. Reboot the device."
Certificate moved to the system store
We recommend using the adguardcert Magisk module
AdGuard will be able to filter HTTPS traffic both in browsers and apps
Move certificate to the system store?
Try deleting it manually via Magisk

"1. Open Magisk → Modules and disable AdGuard Certificate or MoveCerts!. 2. Reboot the device. 3. Open Settings → Biometrics and security → Other security settings. 4.Tap User certificates and delete the AdGuard certificate. These instructions may differ for older or customized versions of Android. If you have trouble locating the certificates, use the search bar or contact the developers of your OS version."
To filter HTTPS traffic, you’ll have to reinstall the certificate
AdGuard won’t be able to filter HTTPS traffic in apps
Failed to open device security settings
Firefox has its own certificate store
Required to filter HTTPS traffic in apps
AdGuard installs its certificates to filter HTTPS traffic. This allows AdGuard to block ads in browsers and apps
AdGuard installs its certificates to filter HTTPS traffic. This allows AdGuard to block ads and trackers in most browsers
Move certificate to the system store

"It helps us understand how users interact with the app and identify its popular features and usability issues.
This data is anonymous and only used internally. We never share it with third parties."
• The use and performance of the app

If you want us to add something new
Send app logs and system info
Enter your email address in the format example@mail.com
Info about running processes, installed apps, memory, CPU, battery state, and app logs
Please tell us what went wrong. When did the problem occur? Do you use a VPN or an antivirus?
If something goes wrong with the app
Most questions are already answered here
If you want us to add something new
If you see a missed ad

To use Watchdog, allow AdGuard to set alarms and reminders
Checks if AdGuard gets shut down by the battery optimization system and tries to restart it
Your changes on this page may not be saved
Downloaded files are not deleted when you clear your browser history

"All tabs will be closed
Website data won’t be cleared — you can clear it after you close the browser"

1. On your home screen, tap and hold an empty space
2. Tap Widgets
3. Select AdGuard Browser
4. Tap and hold the widget, then drag it anywhere
Surf the Web in incognito mode, without ads or trackers. By default, website data is cleared when you close the browser
Go incognito with our private browser

1. Open a video
2. Tap Share and select AdGuard Browser
3. Watch it free of ads and trackers in our secure browser!

To filter DNS requests, disable Private DNS in your device settings

"Unlock Tracking protection with AdGuard's full version. Tap to learn more"
"AdGuard won't let companies track you or sell your data"
Protect yourself from trackers, remove UTM tags and online counters, and more

"Every time you visit a website, AdGuard consults the constantly updated database of malicious and phishing domains. If the website is dangerous, you'll see a warning message.

As AdGuard checks hash prefixes, not website URLs, it doesn't know what websites you visit.

Learn more"
Protect yourself from phishing and malware
Buy the full version to unlock this feature
Blocks requests to malicious and phishing websites

Blocks unwanted ads like banners, videos, and popups

2. Tap Widgets
3. Select AdGuard Browser
4. Tap and hold the widget, then drag it anywhere

Your data is at risk: connection to this website is not encrypted

No stats yet — keep using AdGuard to view them
No stats yet — keep using AdGuard to view them
All requests from Recent activity and stats on data usage will be permanently deleted
AdGuard will show stats for the selected time period
AdGuard will show stats on traffic usage for the selected type of data
Allows you to set your own tracking protection parameters

Removes tracking parameters from page URLs. Limits the lifetime of third-party cookies
You may have to re-log in to some websites or face other cookie-related issues

Limits the lifetime of third-party cookies. Blocks browser APIs
Some websites may get broken or work incorrectly
Prevents websites from detecting your location
Blocks trackers and web analytics using AdGuard Tracking Protection filter

"Blocks WebRTC that can leak your real IP address even if you use a proxy or VPN
Some messengers, streaming platforms, or games may stop working properly"
This feature is available in the full version
"Size of the first ClientHello fragment in bytes (from 1 to 1500)"
"This setting isn't applied because Tracking protection is disabled"

"Prevents websites from remembering your details, such as your login credentials and language preferences, by limiting the lifetime of fist-party cookies
To block the cookies, set the lifetime to 0"
If you block these cookies, some websites may break or stop working

"Prevents third parties from knowing what websites you visit
The HTTP header for requests will be replaced with the one below. To use the default Referer, leave the field blank"

"Removes identifying information from the User-Agent header
Your User-Agent will be replaced with the below info. To use the default User-Agent, leave the field blank"
"Size of the first HTTP request fragment in bytes (from 1 to 1500)"
Varies spacing in HTTP requests to prevent DPI systems from recognizing them
May disrupt access to some unencrypted websites
Increases the size of the first packet in a connection to several kilobytes
Tricks websites into believing you are a proxy with the IP address below. To use the default IP address, leave the field blank
"Splits ClientHellos into two fragments First fragment size: %d"
Splits HTTP requests into two fragments
"Splits HTTP requests into two fragments First fragment size: %d"
"Modifies your outgoing traffic so that your ISP's Deep Packet Inspection systems can't detect the websites you visit. Can't guarantee complete protection against all DPI systems"
Delay before sending the remaining fragment of ClientHello or HTTP request

"Prevents advertisers from tracking your behaviour across multiple pages by limiting the lifetime of third-party cookies
To block the cookies, set the lifetime to 0"
If you block these cookies, you may have to re-log in to some websites or face other cookie-related issues
"This setting isn't applied because Tracking protection is disabled"
Disables cached authorization data that can be used for tracking. May interfere with the functionality of some browser extensions and websites
Removes the ETag and If-None-Match headers from requests and responses to prevent tracking by them
Prevents third parties from knowing what websites you visit
Hides your searches from websites visited through search engine results
Removes identifying information from the User-Agent header
Tricks websites into believing you are a proxy with the IP address below
Buy the full version to unlock this feature
"Modifies your outgoing traffic so that your ISP's Deep Packet Inspection systems can't detect the websites you visit. Can't guarantee complete protection against all DPI systems"
Blocks Google Chrome from sending its version and modifications information to Google domains
Prevents websites from remembering your details, such as your login credentials and language preferences, by limiting the lifetime of first-party cookies
Prevents advertisers from tracking your behaviour across multiple pages by limiting the lifetime of third-party cookies
Sends the Global Privacy Control and Do Not Track signals to the websites you visit
Ask websites not to track me
Protects your online activity and personal data from web trackers
Removes tracking parameters from page URLs using AdGuard URL Tracking filter

"AdGuard frees your Web from clutter. It blocks banners, popups, and home screen ads
However, it can't block ads on YouTube and in other videos"
"AdGuard alerts you if you're about to visit a phishing or malicious website"
AdGuard shields your personal data from apps and websites that try to spy on you. It blocks tracking cookies, analytics systems, and UTM tags

"To be clear: we collect as little data as possible to run our websites and apps. We don't share your data with anyone"

Which apps and companies would potentially leak your data? Now you can view detailed stats for apps, companies, and domains.
"We made the design lighter and easier to understand and put the “core” features on a separate screen — now it's much easier to access them."
"We've significantly improved the stability of the Integration mode. What's more, now you can exclude apps from your proxy — even from AdGuard VPN!"
Check out our blog for more information about the new version.
"We've completely redesigned and rewritten the app — let's see what's changed."
Now you can control the access to the Internet for all your apps — if you wish, prevent them from using the Internet without your knowledge.
"What's new in AdGuard v4.0 for Android"

"This setting isn't applied because %s is disabled"

"This setting isn't applied because DNS protection is disabled"

1. Go to Settings → Apps → Permissions → Background autostart and make sure AdGuard is allowed to run in the background
2. Return to Settings → Apps → Manage apps → AdGuard and uncheck Pause app activity if unused
3. In Battery saver, tap No restrictions

1. Go to Settings → Apps → Manage apps → AdGuard and enable Autostart
2. In Battery saver, tap No restrictions

"Let AdGuard work in the background and protect you even when you close the app. To do this, you need to change your phone’s battery optimization settings
Select the instructions that match your device settings"
